title: In Ukraine, those who leave and those who stay: Life on hold in Sloviansk and Kramatorsk
subhead: 'Donbas: War of Attrition' (2/2). In this industrial region, 80% of which is occupied by Russian troops, the cities of Sloviansk and Kramatorsk have become the heart of the Ukrainian defensive system. Some of their residents choose to flee to safer areas. Others try to survive as best they can.

'I just want to know if he's still alive': The long ordeal of families searching for loved ones lost at sea
subhead: Since the beginning of the year, shipwrecks in the Mediterranean and Atlantic have multiplied, leaving friends and relatives without news of those who attempt the crossing.

title: France counts 74 drowning deaths since June 18 amid record-breaking heatwave
subhead: Much of western Europe has been baking under a 'heat dome' this week, and the extreme conditions have come than is usual this summer.

title: Cultural leaders rally to save French heritage under threat
subhead: The launch of a heritage pass, the search for sponsors and the commitment of volunteers all point to the same effort: those working to preserve France's heritage sites are looking for new ways to avoid a looming funding gap.

title: 250 years of American independence: Frederick Douglass, the former slave turned celebrity who skewered the Republic's hypocrisy
subhead: 'America 250' (6/13). In April 1876, all of Washington's elite gathered to witness the unveiling of a statue of Abraham Lincoln emancipating enslaved people. The main speaker, Frederick Douglass, himself formerly enslaved and one of the most famous faces in the country, was known for directly challenging white Americans. A legendary speech he gave in 1852 proved just that.

Sleeping in a cellar, waiting at a funeral home, riding the tram: How the French are improvising to survive the heatwave
subhead: Residents describe the sometimes unusual strategies they have adopted to endure the heat. Their improvised solutions highlight the shortcomings in climate change adaptation.

title: Mansplashing: Why do men splash everyone at the pool?
subhead: The behavior of the so-called 'pool man,' who thrashes around in the water and sprawls out across swimming lanes, highlights the power dynamics at play in the shared social space of the public swimming pool.

title: Ukraine's Donbas rear area, from Sloviansk to Kramatorsk, is under constant Russian pressure
subhead: 'Donbas: The War of Attrition' (1/2). Just a few kilometers from one of Ukraine's fiercest fronts, these two cities are under drone strikes that make residents' lives very difficult. Both civilians and soldiers fear the bombings will intensify.
byline: Thomas d’Istria and  Virginie NGuyen Hoang
